xanesnet.utils.graph package¶

Public API for XANESNET graph construction utilities.

xanesnet.utils.graph.build_absorber_paths(pmg_obj, absorber_idx, cutoff, max_paths)[source]¶

Enumerate absorber-centred 3-body paths (absorber, j, k).

Both j and k must be within cutoff of the absorber. For periodic structures, j and k may be periodic images; their scalar geometry is computed from pymatgen image Cartesian coordinates. Paths are ordered by ascending r0j + r0k + 0.5 * rjk (a proxy for path significance) and truncated to max_paths per structure.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – The periodic structure or molecule.

  • absorber_idx (int) – Index of the absorbing atom in pmg_obj.

  • cutoff (float) – Neighbor cutoff radius in angstroms.

  • max_paths (int) – Maximum number of paths to return.

Returns:

  • path_j: int64 – structure-global atom index of j.

    • path_k: int64 – structure-global atom index of k.

  • path_r0j: float32 – absorber-j distance in angstroms.

  • path_r0k: float32 – absorber-k distance in angstroms.

  • path_rjk: float32 – j-k distance in angstroms.

  • path_cosangle: float32 – cosine of the angle at the absorber (range [-1, 1]).

Return type:

dict[str, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.build_edges(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, compute_vectors=True, method='radius', min_facet_area=None, cov_radii_scale=1.5)[source]¶

Dispatch edge construction to the requested graph-building method.

Supports periodic Structure and non-periodic Molecule objects uniformly.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – The pymatgen object whose atoms are graph nodes.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms. For "voronoi" this bounds the edge length after tessellation. For "cov_radius" this serves as a hard upper bound (the effective cutoff is the smaller of cutoff and cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst)).

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges kept per source node (shortest first). The returned graph is guaranteed to be bidirectional regardless of this truncation.

  • compute_vectors (bool) – If False, edge_vec is returned as None to save memory in downstream consumers that do not need it. Geometry-aware methods still compute vectors internally (they are needed for symmetrisation).

  • method (str) – One of "radius" (default), "voronoi", or "cov_radius".

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Only used by "voronoi". See build_edges_voronoi.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Only used by "cov_radius". See build_edges_cov_radius.

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64 – source/destination node indices.

  • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge length in angstroms.

  • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 or None.

  • edge_attr: (E,) float32 or None – extra per-edge scalar (facet area for "voronoi", None otherwise).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None, Tensor | None]

Raises:

ValueError – If method is not one of GRAPH_METHODS.

xanesnet.utils.graph.build_edges_cov_radius(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Covalent-radius edge construction.

An edge (i, j) is kept iff d(i, j) <= min(cutoff, cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_i + r_cov_j)), where r_cov values are taken from ASE’s Cordero covalent-radii table. cutoff acts as a hard maximum (useful for speed and for capping extreme element combinations).

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Hard maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges per source node.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Scale factor applied to the sum of covalent radii.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) where edge_attr is always None.

xanesnet.utils.graph.build_edges_radius(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors)[source]¶

Radius-graph edge construction.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges per source node.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) where edge_attr is always None for the radius method.

xanesnet.utils.graph.build_edges_voronoi(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, min_facet_area=None)[source]¶

Voronoi-tessellation edge construction.

Two atoms are connected iff their Voronoi cells share a facet. For periodic structures, the tessellation is computed on a supercell so that facets with periodic images are resolved correctly. edge_weight is always the Cartesian distance of the edge, which equals the correct minimum-image distance for periodic graphs.

After optional facet-area filtering, edges are truncated to max_num_neighbors per source node and symmetrised to guarantee a bidirectional graph.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms. Facets between atoms farther apart are dropped.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional lower bound on the Voronoi facet area. None disables the filter. A float is an absolute threshold in angstroms squared. A str like "1.0%" is a fraction of the largest facet area in the structure.

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors.

    • edge_attr: (E,) float32 – Voronoi facet areas in

    angstroms squared.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.compute_triplets_and_angles(edge_index, edge_vec, num_nodes, is_periodic)[source]¶

Compute triplets (k->j->i) and the bond angle at j.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – directed edge list (source, target).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – displacement vectors pos[target] - pos[source].

  • num_nodes (int) – Total number of nodes in the graph.

  • is_periodic (bool) – If True, degenerate bounce-back triplets are identified by comparing displacement vectors rather than atom indices (periodic images of the same atom have different edge vectors). If False (molecular graph), idx_i == idx_k identifies bounce-backs.

Returns:

  • angle: (T,) float – bond angle at j in radians

    (range [0, pi]).

  • idx_kj: (T,) int64 – edge index of the k->j leg.

  • idx_ji: (T,) int64 – edge index of the j->i leg.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.edges_from_molecule(molecule, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale=None)[source]¶

Build edges for a non-periodic molecule using PyG’s radius_graph.

Bidirectionality is enforced after max_num_neighbors truncation.

Parameters:

  • molecule (Molecule) – Non-periodic pymatgen Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • cov_radii_scale (float | None) – If given, edges with d > cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst) are additionally dropped (cutoff still acts as a hard maximum).

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors

    pos[dst] - pos[src].

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.edges_from_structure(structure, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale=None)[source]¶

Build edges for a periodic structure using pymatgen’s neighbor search.

Correctly handles cutoffs larger than the unit cell by expanding images. Truncation is performed after the full neighbor list is built; edges are then symmetrised so the returned graph is guaranteed to be bidirectional.

Parameters:

  • structure (Structure) – Periodic pymatgen Structure.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • cov_radii_scale (float | None) – If given, edges with d > cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst) are additionally dropped (cutoff still acts as a hard maximum).

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors

    pos[dst] - pos[src].

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

Submodules¶

xanesnet.utils.graph.absorber_paths module¶

Absorber-centred 3-body path enumeration for XANESNET graph inputs.

xanesnet.utils.graph.absorber_paths.build_absorber_paths(pmg_obj, absorber_idx, cutoff, max_paths)[source]¶

Enumerate absorber-centred 3-body paths (absorber, j, k).

Both j and k must be within cutoff of the absorber. For periodic structures, j and k may be periodic images; their scalar geometry is computed from pymatgen image Cartesian coordinates. Paths are ordered by ascending r0j + r0k + 0.5 * rjk (a proxy for path significance) and truncated to max_paths per structure.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – The periodic structure or molecule.

  • absorber_idx (int) – Index of the absorbing atom in pmg_obj.

  • cutoff (float) – Neighbor cutoff radius in angstroms.

  • max_paths (int) – Maximum number of paths to return.

Returns:

  • path_j: int64 – structure-global atom index of j.

    • path_k: int64 – structure-global atom index of k.

  • path_r0j: float32 – absorber-j distance in angstroms.

  • path_r0k: float32 – absorber-k distance in angstroms.

  • path_rjk: float32 – j-k distance in angstroms.

  • path_cosangle: float32 – cosine of the angle at the absorber (range [-1, 1]).

Return type:

dict[str, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.edges module¶

Unified edge-construction dispatcher for XANESNET graph inputs.

xanesnet.utils.graph.edges.build_edges(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, compute_vectors=True, method='radius', min_facet_area=None, cov_radii_scale=1.5)[source]¶

Dispatch edge construction to the requested graph-building method.

Supports periodic Structure and non-periodic Molecule objects uniformly.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – The pymatgen object whose atoms are graph nodes.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms. For "voronoi" this bounds the edge length after tessellation. For "cov_radius" this serves as a hard upper bound (the effective cutoff is the smaller of cutoff and cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst)).

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges kept per source node (shortest first). The returned graph is guaranteed to be bidirectional regardless of this truncation.

  • compute_vectors (bool) – If False, edge_vec is returned as None to save memory in downstream consumers that do not need it. Geometry-aware methods still compute vectors internally (they are needed for symmetrisation).

  • method (str) – One of "radius" (default), "voronoi", or "cov_radius".

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Only used by "voronoi". See build_edges_voronoi.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Only used by "cov_radius". See build_edges_cov_radius.

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64 – source/destination node indices.

  • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge length in angstroms.

  • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 or None.

  • edge_attr: (E,) float32 or None – extra per-edge scalar (facet area for "voronoi", None otherwise).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None, Tensor | None]

Raises:

ValueError – If method is not one of GRAPH_METHODS.

xanesnet.utils.graph.gemnet_indices module¶

GemNet triplet, quadruplet, and mixed-triplet index computation.

xanesnet.utils.graph.gemnet_indices.compute_id_swap(edge_index, edge_vec, decimals=3)[source]¶

For each directed edge (c -> a, v) return the index of its counter-edge (a -> c, -v).

Works for molecular and periodic graphs provided the graph is already symmetrised (every forward edge has a reverse partner, possibly at the same image).

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – source/destination node indices.

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – displacement vectors.

  • decimals (int) – Decimal places used when quantising vectors for matching.

Return type:

Tensor

Returns:

(E,) int64 – for each edge i, the index of its reverse edge.

Raises:

ValueError – If no matching counter-edge is found for any edge.

xanesnet.utils.graph.gemnet_indices.compute_triplets(edge_index, num_nodes)[source]¶

Compute GemNet triplet indices c -> a <- b sharing target atom a.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – directed edge list.

  • num_nodes (int) – Total number of nodes in the graph.

Returns:

  • id3_reduce_ca: (T,) int64 – edge indices of the c -> a leg, sorted ascending so Kidx3 is well-defined.

  • id3_expand_ba: (T,) int64 – edge indices of the b -> a leg.

  • Kidx3: (T,) int64 – ragged inner index within each id3_reduce_ca group ([0, 1, ..., K_i - 1]).

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

Note

Self-loop triplets where the same directed edge serves as both legs are removed. Two edges with the same atom indices but different periodic images yield valid, distinct triplets.

xanesnet.utils.graph.gemnet_indices.compute_quadruplets(edge_index, edge_vec, int_edge_index, int_edge_vec, num_nodes, eps=0.0001)[source]¶

Compute GemNet-Q / GemNet-OC quadruplet indices.

Quadruplets have the form c -> a - b <- d where:

  • (c -> a) and (d -> b) are edges of the main graph (embedding cutoff), and

  • (b -> a) is an edge of the interaction graph (int_cutoff, typically larger).

Degenerate quadruplets (c == b, a == d, c == d at the same periodic image) are filtered using path-vector tests:

  • vec_cb = vec_ca - vec_ba (pos_b - pos_c)

  • vec_ad = -(vec_ba + vec_db) (pos_d - pos_a)

  • vec_cd = vec_ca - vec_ba - vec_db (pos_d - pos_c)

For each identity test, the atom indices must also match; otherwise a near-zero vector from two genuinely different atoms would be incorrectly filtered.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – main-graph directed edges.

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – main-graph displacement vectors.

  • int_edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E_int) int64 – interaction-graph edges.

  • int_edge_vec (Tensor) – (E_int, 3) float – interaction displacement vectors.

  • num_nodes (int) – Total number of nodes.

  • eps (float) – Distance threshold below which two positions are considered identical (used for degeneracy filtering).

Returns:

id4_reduce_ca, id4_expand_db, id4_reduce_cab, id4_expand_abd, id4_reduce_intm_ca, id4_expand_intm_db, id4_reduce_intm_ab, id4_expand_intm_ab, Kidx4.

Return type:

dict[str, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.gemnet_indices.compute_mixed_triplets(main_edge_index, main_edge_vec, other_edge_index, other_edge_vec, num_nodes, to_outedge, eps=0.0001)[source]¶

Compute mixed-triplet indices for GemNet-OC atom-edge / edge-atom interactions.

For each “output” edge (c -> a) in main_edge_index, enumerates all “input” edges in other_edge_index that connect to the same atom (either a or c, depending on to_outedge).

Degenerate self-loop mixed triplets are removed via path-vector tests (same atom AND path vector near zero implies same periodic image).

Parameters:

  • main_edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E_out) int64 – output directed edge list.

  • main_edge_vec (Tensor) – (E_out, 3) float – output displacement vectors.

  • other_edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E_in) int64 – input directed edge list.

  • other_edge_vec (Tensor) – (E_in, 3) float – input displacement vectors.

  • num_nodes (int) – Total number of nodes.

  • to_outedge (bool) – If False (atom-edge / edge-atom case), match input edges to the target atom a of each output edge. If True (GemNet-OC triplet_in case), match input edges to the source atom c of each output edge.

  • eps (float) – Distance threshold for degeneracy filtering.

Return type:

dict[str, Tensor]

Returns:

Dictionary with keys in_ (input edge ids), out (output edge ids), and out_agg (ragged inner index enumerating inputs per output edge).

xanesnet.utils.graph.radius module¶

Radius-graph and covalent-radius edge construction for XANESNET.

xanesnet.utils.graph.radius.edges_from_structure(structure, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale=None)[source]¶

Build edges for a periodic structure using pymatgen’s neighbor search.

Correctly handles cutoffs larger than the unit cell by expanding images. Truncation is performed after the full neighbor list is built; edges are then symmetrised so the returned graph is guaranteed to be bidirectional.

Parameters:

  • structure (Structure) – Periodic pymatgen Structure.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • cov_radii_scale (float | None) – If given, edges with d > cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst) are additionally dropped (cutoff still acts as a hard maximum).

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors

    pos[dst] - pos[src].

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.radius.edges_from_molecule(molecule, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale=None)[source]¶

Build edges for a non-periodic molecule using PyG’s radius_graph.

Bidirectionality is enforced after max_num_neighbors truncation.

Parameters:

  • molecule (Molecule) – Non-periodic pymatgen Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • cov_radii_scale (float | None) – If given, edges with d > cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_src + r_cov_dst) are additionally dropped (cutoff still acts as a hard maximum).

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors

    pos[dst] - pos[src].

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.radius.build_edges_radius(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors)[source]¶

Radius-graph edge construction.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges per source node.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) where edge_attr is always None for the radius method.

xanesnet.utils.graph.radius.build_edges_cov_radius(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, cov_radii_scale)[source]¶

Covalent-radius edge construction.

An edge (i, j) is kept iff d(i, j) <= min(cutoff, cov_radii_scale * (r_cov_i + r_cov_j)), where r_cov values are taken from ASE’s Cordero covalent-radii table. cutoff acts as a hard maximum (useful for speed and for capping extreme element combinations).

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Hard maximum edge length in angstroms.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges per source node.

  • cov_radii_scale (float) – Scale factor applied to the sum of covalent radii.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) where edge_attr is always None.

xanesnet.utils.graph.symmetrize module¶

Edge-list post-processing: per-source truncation and bidirectionality enforcement.

xanesnet.utils.graph.symmetrize.truncate_per_source(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr, max_num_neighbors)[source]¶

Keep at most max_num_neighbors outgoing edges per source node.

Edges are selected greedily by ascending edge_weight (shortest first). The relative ordering of the kept edges is not guaranteed to be preserved.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – source/destination node indices.

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – (E,) float – edge lengths.

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – displacement vectors.

  • edge_attr (Tensor | None) – (E,) float or None – optional per-edge scalar.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum number of outgoing edges to retain per source node. Values <= 0 or None disable truncation.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

Filtered (edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) with the same tensor types as the inputs.

xanesnet.utils.graph.symmetrize.symmetrize_directed_edges(edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr, round_decimals=3)[source]¶

Retain only edges whose reverse counterpart is also present.

An edge (i -> j, vec) is kept iff (j -> i, -vec) also exists in the edge list. edge_vec is used to disambiguate between different periodic images of the same atom pair.

Matching is performed with multiplicity: if one direction appears more times than its reverse after rounding, only the matched pairs are kept.

Coordinates are rounded to round_decimals decimal places to tolerate small numerical differences between forward and reverse vectors produced by independent neighbor searches.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – source/destination node indices.

  • edge_weight (Tensor) – (E,) float – edge lengths.

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – displacement vectors.

  • edge_attr (Tensor | None) – (E,) float or None – optional per-edge scalar.

  • round_decimals (int) – Number of decimal places used when comparing displacement vectors for forward/reverse matching.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor | None]

Returns:

Filtered (edge_index, edge_weight, edge_vec, edge_attr) containing only bidirectional edges.

xanesnet.utils.graph.triplets module¶

Triplet (k->j->i) enumeration and angle computation for SchNet-style models.

xanesnet.utils.graph.triplets.compute_triplets_and_angles(edge_index, edge_vec, num_nodes, is_periodic)[source]¶

Compute triplets (k->j->i) and the bond angle at j.

Parameters:

  • edge_index (Tensor) – (2, E) int64 – directed edge list (source, target).

  • edge_vec (Tensor) – (E, 3) float – displacement vectors pos[target] - pos[source].

  • num_nodes (int) – Total number of nodes in the graph.

  • is_periodic (bool) – If True, degenerate bounce-back triplets are identified by comparing displacement vectors rather than atom indices (periodic images of the same atom have different edge vectors). If False (molecular graph), idx_i == idx_k identifies bounce-backs.

Returns:

  • angle: (T,) float – bond angle at j in radians

    (range [0, pi]).

  • idx_kj: (T,) int64 – edge index of the k->j leg.

  • idx_ji: (T,) int64 – edge index of the j->i leg.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]

xanesnet.utils.graph.voronoi module¶

Voronoi-tessellation edge construction for XANESNET graph inputs.

xanesnet.utils.graph.voronoi.build_edges_voronoi(pmg_obj, cutoff, max_num_neighbors, min_facet_area=None)[source]¶

Voronoi-tessellation edge construction.

Two atoms are connected iff their Voronoi cells share a facet. For periodic structures, the tessellation is computed on a supercell so that facets with periodic images are resolved correctly. edge_weight is always the Cartesian distance of the edge, which equals the correct minimum-image distance for periodic graphs.

After optional facet-area filtering, edges are truncated to max_num_neighbors per source node and symmetrised to guarantee a bidirectional graph.

Parameters:

  • pmg_obj (Structure | Molecule) – Periodic Structure or non-periodic Molecule.

  • cutoff (float) – Maximum edge length in angstroms. Facets between atoms farther apart are dropped.

  • max_num_neighbors (int) – Maximum outgoing edges retained per source node (shortest first).

  • min_facet_area (float | str | None) – Optional lower bound on the Voronoi facet area. None disables the filter. A float is an absolute threshold in angstroms squared. A str like "1.0%" is a fraction of the largest facet area in the structure.

Returns:

  • edge_index: (2, E) int64.

    • edge_weight: (E,) float32 – edge lengths in angstroms.

    • edge_vec: (E, 3) float32 – displacement vectors.

    • edge_attr: (E,) float32 – Voronoi facet areas in

    angstroms squared.

Return type:

tuple[Tensor, Tensor, Tensor, Tensor]
